title: Trapping Rain Water
problem_statement: Compute how much water can be trapped after raining given elevation heights.

examples.explanation: Six units of water are trapped between the bars.

intuition: Use two pointers and keep track of the max heights from each end to compute trapped water in one pass.

approaches.name: Two-pointer elevation scan
approaches.description: Move the pointer at the lower side inward and add trapped water based on the smaller max height seen so far.
approaches.why_not: Using extra arrays for left and right max heights works but uses extra space.
interview_commentary: The lower side pointer determines trapped water because the current height is bounded by the shorter wall.
Keep track of max heights seen from both ends to compute volume without extra arrays.
Each position is handled exactly once, making the algorithm linear.

interview_qa.q: Why is the lower side pointer moved?
interview_qa.a: Because the trapped water depends on the shorter boundary.

interview_qa.q: What do leftMax and rightMax represent?
interview_qa.a: The highest bar seen so far from each side.
